latin: Dominus Dursley mane ad laborem ibat. in via multas res miras vidit. felem tabulam legentem videre visus est. homines palliis longis induti per urbem ambulabant. bubones multi per caelum clarum volabant. Dominus Dursley perturbatus erat, sed nihil dicere voluit.

questions.prompt: What was Mr. Dursley doing when he noticed strange things?
What animals were flying through the clear sky?
What were the strange people in the city wearing?
How did Mr. Dursley react to the strange sights?
questions.target: He was disturbed but said nothing
choices: He was disturbed but said nothing

translation: Mr. Dursley was going to work in the morning. On the road he saw many strange things. He seemed to see a cat reading a sign. People dressed in long cloaks were walking through the city. Many owls were flying through the clear sky. Mr. Dursley was disturbed, but he did not want to say anything.
